title: Monthly Wellness Subscription Card Liveness Validation Chain
narrative: Fraudster establishes recurring $4.99 monthly subscription to health/wellness merchant (MCC 5817) to maintain card validation over extended period. Initial authorization on stolen card credentials uses temporary email domain (guerrillamail, tempmail). Card successfully processes same $4.99 charge on 28th of each month for 3-6 months while fraudster monetizes card elsewhere for high-value purchases. Low-friction merchant auto-renews without CVV re-verification. Pattern surfaces when cardholder disputes accumulated $29.94-$34.93 in subscription charges months later, revealing card remained active entire period. Fraudster exploits merchant's lenient retry logic and absence of behavioral monitoring on sub-$5 recurring transactions. Transaction metadata shows consistent device_id but rotating IP addresses across billing cycles, indicating automated renewal monitoring rather than legitimate subscriber behavior.
indicators: Identical $4.99 TransactionAmt recurring exactly 28-31 days apart for 3+ cycles
P_emaildomain matches disposable provider list (guerrillamail.com, tempmail.net, 10minutemail.com)
card1_txn_count_30d shows 8-15 other merchants between subscription renewals
ProductCD='W' (wellness/subscription) with MCC 5817, no CVV verification after initial transaction
device_id consistent across renewals but dist1 (distance between billing/IP location) varies 500+ km per cycle
txn_hour for renewals always 02:00-04:00 UTC suggesting automated retry window
card1_amt_zscore shows bimodal distribution: $4.99 recurring plus $200-$800 purchases at unrelated MCCs
summary: A stolen card processes the same $4.99 wellness subscription charge on the 28th of each month for 3-6 months, using a disposable email address and consistent device ID but rotating IP locations 500+ km apart each cycle, while 8-15 other merchants appear between renewals.
reasoning: The fraudster maintains a low-value recurring charge to confirm the card stays active for months while making high-value purchases elsewhere, exploiting merchants that don't re-verify CVV and don't monitor sub-$5 subscriptions for fraud signals.
indicator_explanations: Same tiny charge repeats like clockwork every month to test card validity
Email address comes from throwaway temporary mail services favored by fraudsters
Card shows legitimate-looking activity at multiple other merchants between test charges
Wellness subscription skips security checks after first payment making renewals invisible
Device stays the same but billing location jumps hundreds of miles each renewal
Renewals always fire during dead-of-night hours consistent with automated bot behavior
Card alternates between tiny subscription charges and large unrelated purchases elsewhere
